author: draganrakita
content: On EIP-8038 we should talk what do to with call stipend and sstore stipend guard. Idea for it was that callee can trigger a log if needed but with transfer logs already added in Amsterdam this looks silly. Should we completely remove call stipend and sstore stipend guard?

author: m0_ox
content: This adds some new boundary to BALs as without the stipend you cannot do the implicit `sload` on `sstore` (read doesn't show in BAL if not enough gas for stipend). So we'd have to address this new boundary condition that is no longer based on whether you have the stipend or not.

author: m0_ox
content: Is there a benefit to removing these?

author: draganrakita
content: I dont follow this, stipend does not effect `sload` but just `sstore` and with 2d gas (state gas) it is not effective any more, so there is not a lot of propose there. About BAL it should be similar to sload first static gas then dynamic one.

author: m0_ox
content: Currently,  for `SSTORE`, if you don't have enough for the stipend then you don't even [read] the slot to determine the starting value. Without the stipend, the [read] happens every time. This is what I mean.

author: m0_ox
content: sorry by implicit sload I meant implicit read 🙂

author: m0_ox
content: Yeah I see, this is replaced with the cold / warm check which becomes the new boundary. I see the reasoning now. We just remove the stipend boundary from BAL test cases and check gas for cold / warm instead. I don't think this is a big change in the tests.
